Calculates surface area of Sun [using 퐴=4π푟 ] Use of 퐿=휎퐴푇 Use of 퐼= 퐿 4π푑 퐼=0.89 W m −2 Example of calculation 퐴=4π× ( 6.96×10 m ) =6.09×10 m 퐿=5.67×10 −8 W m −2 K −4 ×6.09×10 m × ( 5800 K ) =3.91×10 W 퐼= 3.91×10 W 4π× ( 5.91×10 m ) =0.891 W m −2 (1) (1) (1) (1) Equates 퐹= 퐺푀푚 푟 with 퐹=푚휔 푟 Use of 휔= 2π 푇 Conversion of T to years 푇=250 (years) OR Use of 퐹= 퐺푀푚 푟 with 퐹= 푚푣 푟 Use of 푣= 2π푟 푇 Conversion of T to years 푇=250 (years) Example of calculation 퐺푀푚 푟 =푚휔 푟 휔= √ 퐺푀 푟 = √ 6.67×10 −11 N m kg −1 ×1.99 ×10 kg ( 5.91 × 10 m ) ∴ω=8.02×10 −10 rad 푠 −1 푇= 2π 휔 = 2π rad 8.02×10 −10 rad s −1 =7.84×10 s= 7.84×10 s 3.15 × 10 s year −1 =249 year (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 16 8 Number Answer Mark Or The weight of the astronaut acts as a centripetal force Or The astronauts are in free fall as they orbit the Earth They do not experience (normal) contact forces (1) (1)

Use of ∆퐹=−푘∆푥 Use of 푇=2π √ 푚 푘 to calculate mass of chair Use of 푇=2π √ 푚 푘 to calculate mass of chair plus astronaut Mass difference calculated Mass of astronaut = 67 kg Example of calculation 푘= 175 N 0.289 m =606 N m −1 푚= 푘푇 4π = 606 N m −1 × ( 0.858 s ) 4π =11.3 kg 푚= 606 N m −1 × ( 2.26 s ) 4π =78.3 kg mass of astronaut= ( 78.3−11.3 ) kg=67.0 kg (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 17 7 Number Answer Mark

Use of 푣=퐻 푑 Use of 푡= 퐻 Conversion from s to year 푡=1.37×10 year Example of calculation 퐻 = 2.02×10 m s −1 8.70×10 m =2.32×10 −18 s −1 푡= 2.32×10 −18 s −1 =4.31×10 s 푡= 4.31×10 s 3.15×10 s year −1 =1.37×10 year (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 18 11 Number Answer Mark The binding energy increases, (so energy is released) [Accept binding energy per nucleon increases] Or There is a decrease in (total) mass, (so energy is released) (1) (1)

Binding energy per nucleon read from graph for before and after fission Binding energy before and after fission calculated Or Binding energy increase calculated Energy released ≈ 240 (MeV) Example of calculation Increase in binding energy per nucleon = (8.5 – 7.5) MeV = 1.0 MeV Increase in binding energy ≈ 238 × 1.0 MeV Energy released ≈ 240 MeV (1) (1) (1) Bottom line correct Example of completed equation Sr→Y +β − −1 +ν̅ e (1) (1) Conversion between eV and J P = 110 (W) Example of calculation 푃=1295×10 s −1 ×0.546 ×10 eV×1.6×10 −19 J eV −1 =113 W (1) (1) (1) Use of 휆= ln2 푡 ½ Use of 퐴=퐴 푒 −휆푡 t = 44 (years) ≠ 50 (years), so claim inaccurate Or (After 50 years), A = 1.1 × 10 15 ( Bq) ≠ 1.295 × 10 15 ( Bq) so claim inaccurate Or (If A 1.295 × 10 Bq ), 퐴 =4.3×10 (Bq) ≠ 3.7 × 10 (Bq) so claim inaccurate Example of calculation 휆= ln2 28.8 =0.0241 years −1 1295 TBq=3700 TBq×e −0.0241 years −1 × 푡 푡= ln( 1295 TBq 3700 TBq ⁄) −0.0241 years −1 =43.6 years (1) (1) (1) Total for question 19 13 Number Answer Mark No change in count rate when paper placed (between source and GM tube), so there can’t be any alpha radiation There can’t be any gamma radiation, as gamma radiation would pass through the aluminium sheet Or There can’t be any gamma radiation, as count rate decreases to background with aluminium sheet. It must be beta radiation as beta radiation would not pass through the aluminium sheet. (1) (1) (1) (1)
